item.title: Rapid diagnosis and therapeutic mitigation in inflammatory bowel disease: utilizing the window of opportunity-findings from the UAE Epi-IBD study.

item.title: Self-Efficacy as a Mediator Between Medication Adherence and Self-Care in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Multicenter Cross-Sectional Study.
item.description: Journal of clinical nursing - To examine the role of self-efficacy in the relationship between medication adherence and self-care behaviours in patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease by describing their levels and exploring t...

item.title: Biologics and small molecules for perianal Crohn's disease: a systematic review and meta-analyses of randomized placebo-controlled trials.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Although several advanced therapies (ATs) for luminal Crohn's disease (CD) have been approved, their efficacy for perianal fistulizing Crohn's (pCD) has not been comprehensively reviewed. A literat...

item.title: [Appendectomy as a disease-modifying therapy in ulcerative colitis?].
item.description: Zeitschrift fur Gastroenterologie - Ulcerative colitis is a chronic inflammatory systemic disease with rising incidence and increased mortality. Despite established therapeutic strategies, particularly maintenance therapy with mesala...

item.title: From preclinical phase of Crohn's disease to postoperative recurrence: shared mechanisms and potential interventions.
item.description: Gut - Review - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic inflammatory disorder of the gastrointestinal tract with high risk of surgery. Despite advances in medical treatment, of those patients who undergo ileocolonic res...

item.title: Synchronous Obstructed Right Para-Duodenal Hernia, and Lower Gastrointestinal Hemorrhage due to Crohn's Ileitis.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - We report a 55-year-old man who presented with acute small bowel obstruction and hematochezia. He was receiving medications for type 2 diabetes mellitus, chronic renal disease, and ischemic heart d...

item.title: Turnbull procedure-analysis of a cohort in the salvage setting.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - The Turnbull procedure (coloanal pull-through with delayed coloanal anastomosis, DCAA) serves as a salvage option for complex, therapy-refractory pelvic floor disorders to avoid permanent colostomy...

item.title: Impact of advanced therapy initiation on antibiotic dependence in chronic pouchitis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - A quarter of patients with ulcerative colitis who undergo total proctocolectomy with ileal pouch-anal anastomosis experience chronic pouchitis. While advanced therapies (ATs) are effective in some ...

item.title: Interpretation of surgical recurrence in the dynamic phenotype of Crohn's disease.
A propensity score-matched NSQIP analysis comparing open, laparoscopic, and robotic approaches for total abdominal colectomy with end ileostomy in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Minimally invasive surgical techniques have improved outcomes in colorectal surgery, but comparative data on their use in total abdominal colectomy with end ileostomy for inflammatory bowel disease...

item.title: Endoscopic recurrence of Crohn's disease following laparoscopic versus robotic ileocolic resection.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Robotic approach is increasingly being used for surgery in Crohn's disease. Robotic ileocolic resection (ICR) for Crohn's disease (CD) is comparable to laparoscopic ICR for short term outcomes; how...

item.title: [Textbook outcome after ileoanal pouch operation for ulcerative colitis: results of nationwide multicenter study].
10 Strategies for prevention of perineal wound dehiscence after intersphincteric proctectomy for perianal Crohn's disease.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Non-healing perineal wounds (NHPWs), including persistent presacral sinuses, remain a common and morbid complication after elective intersphincteric proctectomy (ISP) for fistulizing perianal Crohn...

item.title: Organ Preservation After Total Neoadjuvant Therapy in a Patient With Low Rectal Adenocarcinoma and Crohn's Disease.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a relative contraindication to radiation. This case report describes a 53-year-old man with colorectal adenocarcinoma and a history of Crohn's disease who was tr...

item.title: ASSOCIATED FACTORS AND CLINICAL ASPECTS OF COLECTOMY IN PATIENTS WITH ULCERATIVE COLITIS - EXPERIENCE OF A BRAZILIAN SOUTHEASTERN COHORT.
item.description: Arquivos de gastroenterologia - Observational study - Despite recent advances in the medical treatment of ulcerative colitis (UC) involving the use of biological agents and small molecules, a proportion of patients with UC still require surgical treat...

item.title: A rare and severe case of toxic megacolon: Radiological emergency.
item.description: Radiology case reports - Case report - Toxic megacolon is a rare and life-threatening complication of ulcerative colitis, representing a medical and surgical emergency with a poor prognosis. We report the case of a 31-year-old man with ...
